spec_match.rationale: Exercises anchor-size() in BOTH inset (`bottom: anchor(top)`) and margin (`margin-bottom: anchor-size(height)`) plus sizing (`width: anchor-size(width)`) on a single tooltip — the exact spec change.

interactive.rationale: Slider rescales the anchor live; the tooltip width and offset rescale via pure CSS without any JS layout maths — proves the headline benefit.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'anchor-size() usable in inset/margin properties' — exactly delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: No @supports gate / fallback (pre-131 browsers will fail silently with the tooltip in the wrong place), no demo of multiplicative `calc()` expressions in margin (the spec change permits `calc(anchor-size(width) * 0.5)` style usage), and no inverse-direction edge case.

references.rationale: See also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5203950077476864 and the CSS spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, no static-card pattern, tip uses --accent-blue/--bg-ivory pair (passes WCAG AA).

openQuestions.title: Add an @supports gate with a visible fallback
openQuestions.detail: Pre-131 browsers parse the anchor-size() in inset/margin properties as invalid, so the tooltip silently jumps to (0,0) on those browsers. Add `@supports (margin-bottom: anchor-size(height))` and a fallback that pins the tooltip with hardcoded values, plus a visible 'no support detected' banner.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate calc() / multiplicative inset
openQuestions.detail: The headline use-case is `calc(anchor-size(width) * 0.5)` style inset proportions. The current page uses only `anchor-size(height)` directly. Add a second tooltip / preset showing `top: calc(anchor(bottom) + anchor-size(height) * 0.25)` so the multiplier pattern is visible.

summary: Clean tooltip demo that hits the spec change in one expressive snippet. Could explicitly handle the unsupported-browser case and showcase the multiplicative `calc()` pattern the spec really enables.
